title: Area Word Problems
essential_question: How can you use what you know about area to solve real-world problems?

pretest.question: Tom's bedroom floor is 9 ft long and 8 ft wide. What is its area?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.12 Lesson — U12_area/L7_area_word_problems
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: Lily's poster is 6 in × 12 in. Area in sq in?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.12 Lesson — U12_area/L7_area_word_problems
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: Sara's garden is 5 m × 4 m. Mia's garden is 3 m × 7 m. Whose garden has a BIGGER area? (Write Sara or Mia.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.12 Lesson — U12_area/L7_area_word_problems
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: A rug has area 24 sq ft and length 6 ft. What is its width? (Write number of ft.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.12 Lesson — U12_area/L7_area_word_problems
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: Tom has 36 square tiles. Which arrangement makes a rectangle?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.12 Lesson — U12_area/L7_area_word_problems
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Read the Problem
learn.content: In an area word problem, find: (1) WHAT shape? (rectangle, square, etc.) (2) WHAT dimensions are given? (3) WHAT is being asked? (area, missing side, comparison?)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.12 Lesson — U12_area/L7_area_word_problems
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Find the Area
learn.content: When asked for area: find length and width, then multiply. Don't forget the SQUARE units (sq cm, sq m, sq ft).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.12 Lesson — U12_area/L7_area_word_problems
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Find a Missing Side
learn.content: If you know the area and one side, divide: missing side = area ÷ known side. Example: area 18 sq cm, length 6 cm → width = 18 ÷ 6 = 3 cm.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.12 Lesson — U12_area/L7_area_word_problems
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Compare Two Shapes
learn.content: When asked to compare areas: find each area, then compare the numbers. Whichever is larger has the larger area.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.12 Lesson — U12_area/L7_area_word_problems
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Combined Areas
learn.content: If a problem has two rooms or pieces, find each area and ADD them (if they don't overlap). Example: living room 4×6 = 24 plus dining 3×4 = 12. Total = 36 sq m.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.12 Lesson — U12_area/L7_area_word_problems
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Area Minus Removed
learn.content: If something is REMOVED (cut, hole, etc.), subtract: usable area = full area − removed area. Example: floor 8×6 = 48 with 2×2 = 4 hole → usable = 44.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.12 Lesson — U12_area/L7_area_word_problems
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Estimate First
learn.content: Before solving, ESTIMATE the answer. A 9×8 rectangle should be about 72 sq units — definitely more than 50, less than 100. If your final answer is 17, you know something is wrong.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.12 Lesson — U12_area/L7_area_word_problems
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Lesson Summary
learn.content: Today: (1) Read carefully — what shape, what's given, what's asked. (2) Area = length × width. (3) Missing side = area ÷ known side. (4) Compare by finding both areas first. (5) Add for combined; subtract for removed. (6) Use square units. (7) Estimate first.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.12 Lesson — U12_area/L7_area_word_problems
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
